selected_sql: SELECT      p.patient_id,     p.first_name,     p.last_name FROM lab_tests lt JOIN patients p ON lt.patient_id = p.patient_id WHERE lt.test_status = 'completed' GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.first_name, p.last_name HAVING CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN lt.abnormal_flag IN ('critical', 'high', 'low') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) > (     SELECT AVG(abnormal_pct)     FROM (         SELECT
audit_reason: Filters to patients above the average abnormal rate instead of returning each patient's abnormal-test percentage.

question: How many distinct doctors both recorded a diagnosis and prescribed medication?
selected_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT d.doctor_id) FROM doctors d JOIN diagnoses diag ON d.doctor_id = diag.doctor_id JOIN prescriptions p ON d.doctor_id = p.doctor_id JOIN billing_claims bc ON d.doctor_id = bc.doctor_id
audit_reason: Adds an unrequested billing-claim join, excluding doctors who diagnosed and prescribed without a claim.

question: How many distinct insurance providers paid claims for both procedures and prescriptions?
selected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT "insurance_policies"."provider_name", COUNT(*) AS "claim_count" FROM "billing_claims" INNER JOIN "appointments" ON "billing_claims"."appointment_id" = "appointments"."appointment_id" INNER JOIN "insurance_policies" ON "billing_claims"."policy_id" = "insurance_policies"."policy_id" INNER JOIN "prescriptions" ON "appointments"."appointment_id" = "prescriptions"."appointment_id" INNER
audit_reason: Returns provider rows rather than one count, never filters paid claims, and requires procedures and prescriptions on the same appointment.

question: How many distinct patients had both severe and mild diagnoses?
selected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT "diagnoses"."patient_id", COUNT(*) AS "severe_count", COUNT(*) AS "mild_count" FROM "diagnoses" GROUP BY "diagnoses"."patient_id" HAVING "severe_count" > ? AND "mild_count" > ?
audit_reason: Uses identical unfiltered counts for severe and mild diagnoses and returns rows instead of one count.

question: How many distinct departments handled both urgent and routine appointments?
selected_sql: SELECT "departments"."department_id", "departments"."department_name", COUNT("appointments"."appointment_id") AS "urgent_count", COUNT("appointments"."appointment_id") AS "routine_count" FROM "appointments" INNER JOIN "departments" ON "appointments"."department_id" = "departments"."department_id" GROUP BY "departments"."department_id", "departments"."department_name" HAVING "urgent_count" > ? AND
audit_reason: Uses identical unfiltered counts for urgent and routine appointments and returns rows instead of one count.

question: Show active diagnosis count by doctor.
selected_sql: SELECT "doctors"."doctor_id", "doctors"."first_name", "doctors"."last_name", COUNT(*) AS "diagnosis_count" FROM "doctors" INNER JOIN "diagnoses" ON "doctors"."doctor_id" = "diagnoses"."doctor_id" WHERE "doctors"."active_flag" = ? GROUP BY "doctors"."doctor_id", "doctors"."first_name", "doctors"."last_name"
audit_reason: Filters active doctors rather than diagnoses with clinical_status = 'active'.

question: Show complication count by procedure category.
selected_sql: SELECT procedure_category, COUNT(*) AS complication_count FROM procedures GROUP BY procedure_category HAVING SUM(complication_flag) > 0
audit_reason: Counts every procedure in categories having any complication rather than counting only complicated procedures.

question: Show diagnosis count and prescription cost by patient blood type.
selected_sql: WITH diagnosis_counts AS (     SELECT          patient_id,         COUNT(DISTINCT diagnosis_id) AS diagnosis_count     FROM diagnoses     GROUP BY patient_id ), prescription_costs AS (     SELECT          patient_id,         SUM(estimated_cost) AS total_prescription_cost     FROM prescriptions     GROUP BY patient_id ) SELECT      p.blood_type,     SUM(dc.diagnosis_count) AS diagnosis_count,     S
audit_reason: Inner-joining the patient-level diagnosis and prescription aggregates excludes patients having only one of the two.

question: List departments that completed more than two procedures.
selected_sql: SELECT d.department_id, d.department_name FROM departments d JOIN procedures p ON d.department_id = p.department_id GROUP BY d.department_id HAVING COUNT(p.procedure_id) > 2
audit_reason: Never requires procedure_status = 'completed'.

question: List appointments where the appointment department differs from the doctor's department.
selected_sql: SELECT "appointments"."appointment_id", "appointments"."department_id", "doctors"."department_id" FROM "appointments" INNER JOIN "doctors" ON "appointments"."doctor_id" = "doctors"."doctor_id"
audit_reason: Returns both department IDs but never filters for inequality.

question: List departments with completed appointments, procedures, and billing claims.
selected_sql: SELECT d.department_id, d.department_name FROM departments d JOIN appointments a ON d.department_id = a.department_id JOIN procedures p ON a.appointment_id = p.appointment_id JOIN billing_claims bc ON a.appointment_id = bc.appointment_id WHERE a.appointment_status = 'completed' GROUP BY d.department_id, d.department_name HAVING COUNT(CASE WHEN p.procedure_status = 'completed' THEN 1 END) > 0    AN
audit_reason: Adds unrequested completed-procedure and approved-claim requirements.

question: List doctors who both prescribed medication and performed a procedure for the same appointment.
selected_sql: SELECT d.doctor_id, d.first_name, d.last_name FROM doctors d JOIN appointments a ON d.doctor_id = a.doctor_id JOIN prescriptions p ON a.appointment_id = p.appointment_id JOIN procedures pr ON a.appointment_id = pr.appointment_id GROUP BY d.doctor_id, d.first_name, d.last_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT a.appointment_id) > 0
audit_reason: Uses the appointment doctor but never requires that doctor to author the prescription and perform the procedure.

question: Show the top 10 departments by completed procedure count.
selected_sql: SELECT d.department_id, d.department_name FROM departments d JOIN procedures p ON d.department_id = p.department_id GROUP BY d.department_id, d.department_name ORDER BY COUNT(p.procedure_id) DESC LIMIT 10
audit_reason: Ranks departments by all procedures and never filters procedure_status = 'completed'.

question: List departments with both abnormal lab tests and completed procedures but no denied claims.
selected_sql: SELECT "departments"."department_id", "departments"."department_name", COUNT(*) AS "abnormal_lab_count", COUNT(*) AS "completed_proc_count", COUNT(*) AS "denied_claim_count" FROM "departments" INNER JOIN "appointments" ON "departments"."department_id" = "appointments"."department_id" INNER JOIN "billing_claims" ON "appointments"."appointment_id" = "billing_claims"."appointment_id" INNER JOIN "lab_
audit_reason: Uses identical unfiltered counts for abnormal labs, completed procedures, and denied claims.

question: List patients who had both urgent and routine appointments but no telehealth appointment.
selected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT "patients"."patient_id", "patients"."first_name", "patients"."last_name", COUNT(*) AS "urgent_count", COUNT(*) AS "routine_count" FROM "patients" INNER JOIN "appointments" ON "patients"."patient_id" = "appointments"."patient_id" WHERE "appointments"."telehealth_flag" = ? GROUP BY "patients"."patient_id" HAVING "urgent_count" >= ? AND "routine_count" >= ?
audit_reason: Uses identical unfiltered counts for urgent and routine appointments.

question: List laboratories that produced both high and low abnormal results but no critical results.
selected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT "lab_tests"."laboratory_name", COUNT(*) AS "high_count", COUNT(*) AS "low_count", COUNT(*) AS "critical_count" FROM "lab_tests" GROUP BY "lab_tests"."laboratory_name" HAVING "high_count" > ? AND "low_count" > ? AND "critical_count" = ?
audit_reason: Uses identical unfiltered counts for high, low, and critical results.

question: List patients who had appointments in both medical and surgical departments but no diagnostic department.
selected_sql: SELECT p.patient_id, p.first_name, p.last_name FROM patients p JOIN appointments a ON p.patient_id = a.patient_id JOIN departments d ON a.department_id = d.department_id WHERE d.specialty_category IN ('medical', 'surgical') GROUP BY p.patient_id, p.first_name, p.last_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN d.specialty_category = 'medical' THEN d.department_id END) > 0    AND COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN
audit_reason: Filters out diagnostic departments before HAVING, making the no-diagnostic condition automatically true.

question: What is the average market value of players who play as forwards?
selected_sql: SELECT AVG(players.market_value) FROM players WHERE players.position = 'forward'
audit_reason: Filters position = 'forward', but the database stores the value as 'Forward', so the equality is not value-grounded.

question: What is the average number of completed matches per active season?
selected_sql: SELECT AVG(match_count) AS average_matches FROM (     SELECT s.season_id, COUNT(m.match_id) AS match_count     FROM seasons s     JOIN matches m ON s.season_id = m.season_id     WHERE s.season_status = 'active'     GROUP BY s.season_id )
audit_reason: Counts every match in active seasons and never filters matches to completed status.

question: How many active players have both a current contract and at least one completed transfer?
selected_sql: SELECT "players"."player_id", "players"."active_flag", "contracts"."contract_status", "players".*, COUNT("transfers"."transfer_id") AS "transfer_count" FROM "players" INNER JOIN "contracts" ON "players"."player_id" = "contracts"."player_id" INNER JOIN "transfers" ON "players"."player_id" = "transfers"."player_id" WHERE "players"."active_flag" = ? AND "contracts"."contract_status" = ? GROUP BY "pla
audit_reason: Returns grouped player rows instead of one count and never requires transfer_status = 'completed'.

question: Calculate the average coach win percentage for each coach role.
selected_sql: SELECT coach_role, AVG(CAST(wins AS REAL) / (wins + losses + draws)) AS avg_win_percentage FROM coaches GROUP BY coach_role HAVING wins + losses + draws > 0
audit_reason: Applies the nonzero denominator test in HAVING with non-grouped coach columns, so roles are filtered using an arbitrary row rather than valid per-coach percentages.

question: For each coach, calculate completed team matches per year of coaching experience.
selected_sql: SELECT c.coach_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, c.years_coaching, COUNT(m.match_id) * 1.0 / c.years_coaching AS completed_matches_per_year FROM coaches c JOIN teams t ON c.team_id = t.team_id JOIN matches m ON (m.home_team_id = t.team_id OR m.away_team_id = t.team_id) WHERE c.years_coaching > 0 GROUP BY c.coach_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, c.years_coaching HAVING COUNT(m.match_id) > 0
audit_reason: Counts every team match and never filters match_status = 'completed'.

question: How many distinct players have training performance records?
selected_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT pts.player_id) FROM player_training_stats pts JOIN teams t ON pts.team_id = t.team_id JOIN coaches c ON t.team_id = c.team_id
audit_reason: Adds an unnecessary coach join, excluding training-record players whose teams have no coach record.

question: How many distinct players completed a transfer?
selected_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT player_id) FROM transfers
audit_reason: Counts every transferred player and never filters transfer_status = 'completed'.

question: How many distinct players have both a contract and a training record?
selected_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.player_id) FROM players p JOIN contracts c ON p.player_id = c.player_id JOIN player_training_stats pts ON p.player_id = pts.player_id JOIN teams t ON p.team_id = t.team_id JOIN transfers tr ON p.player_id = tr.player_id
audit_reason: Adds an unrequested transfer join, requiring a transfer in addition to the requested contract and training record.

question: How many distinct teams have both completed matches and completed transfers?
selected_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT t.team_id) FROM teams t JOIN leagues l ON t.league_id = l.league_id JOIN seasons s ON s.league_id = l.league_id JOIN matches m ON m.season_id = s.season_id AND (m.home_team_id = t.team_id OR m.away_team_id = t.team_id) JOIN players p ON p.team_id = t.team_id JOIN transfers tr ON tr.player_id = p.player_id AND tr.season_id = s.season_id
audit_reason: Never filters completed matches or completed transfers and links transfers through current-roster players rather than the team's transfer roles.

question: How many distinct players have played matches, attended training, and completed a transfer?
selected_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT p.player_id) FROM players p JOIN player_match_stats pms ON p.player_id = pms.player_id JOIN player_training_stats pts ON p.player_id = pts.player_id JOIN transfers t ON p.player_id = t.player_id JOIN seasons s ON t.season_id = s.season_id JOIN teams tm ON p.team_id = tm.team_id
audit_reason: Never requires a completed transfer or an attended training status; any transfer and any training record qualify.

question: Show completed match count by league and season year.
selected_sql: SELECT l.league_name, s.season_year, COUNT(m.match_id) AS completed_match_count FROM leagues l JOIN seasons s ON l.league_id = s.league_id JOIN matches m ON s.season_id = m.season_id GROUP BY l.league_name, s.season_year
audit_reason: Counts all matches by league and year and never filters match_status = 'completed'.

question: List teams that completed more transfers than the average team.
selected_sql: SELECT t.team_id, t.team_name FROM teams t JOIN transfers tr ON t.team_id = tr.to_team_id OR t.team_id = tr.from_team_id GROUP BY t.team_id, t.team_name HAVING COUNT(tr.transfer_id) > (     SELECT AVG(transfer_count)     FROM (         SELECT COUNT(*) AS transfer_count         FROM transfers         GROUP BY player_id     ) )
audit_reason: Never filters completed transfers and compares team transfer counts with averages grouped by player rather than by team.

question: List training sessions attended by players who also have match appearances.
selected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT ts.training_session_id, ts.session_date, ts.session_type, pts.player_id FROM training_sessions ts JOIN player_training_stats pts ON ts.training_session_id = pts.training_session_id JOIN player_match_stats pms ON pts.player_id = pms.player_id
audit_reason: Treats any training performance row as attendance and never requires attendance_status = 'present'.

question: List players who scored in a completed match, attended high-intensity training, and have an active contract.
selected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT p.player_id, p.first_name, p.last_name FROM players p JOIN contracts c ON p.player_id = c.player_id JOIN player_match_stats pms ON p.player_id = pms.player_id JOIN matches m ON pms.match_id = m.match_id JOIN player_training_stats pts ON p.player_id = pts.player_id JOIN training_sessions ts ON pts.training_session_id = ts.training_session_id WHERE c.contract_status = 'active'   AND
audit_reason: Treats any training record as attendance and never requires attendance_status = 'present'.

question: List players who transferred between teams in the same league and later played for the destination team.
selected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT "players"."player_id", "players"."first_name", "players"."last_name" FROM "transfers" INNER JOIN "players" ON "transfers"."player_id" = "players"."player_id" INNER JOIN "seasons" ON "transfers"."season_id" = "seasons"."season_id" INNER JOIN "leagues" ON "seasons"."league_id" = "leagues"."league_id" INNER JOIN "teams" ON "players"."team_id" = "teams"."team_id" WHERE "transfers"."tra
audit_reason: Does not compare former and destination league IDs, does not verify a later destination-team appearance, and adds an unrelated active-league condition.

question: List active players whose current team differs from both their contract team and latest match team.
selected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT p.player_id, p.first_name, p.last_name, p.team_id FROM players p JOIN contracts c ON p.player_id = c.player_id JOIN player_match_stats pms ON p.player_id = pms.player_id JOIN matches m ON pms.match_id = m.match_id WHERE p.current_status = 'active'   AND p.team_id != c.team_id   AND p.team_id != (     SELECT pms2.team_id     FROM player_match_stats pms2     JOIN matches m2 ON pms2.m
audit_reason: Adds an unrequested requirement that the contract team also differ from the latest match team.

question: List players who appeared for one team, trained with another team, and transferred to a third team.
selected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT "players"."player_id", "players"."first_name", "players"."last_name" FROM "players" INNER JOIN "player_match_stats" ON "players"."player_id" = "player_match_stats"."player_id" INNER JOIN "player_training_stats" ON "players"."player_id" = "player_training_stats"."player_id" INNER JOIN "transfers" ON "players"."player_id" = "transfers"."player_id"
audit_reason: Never enforces three different teams and never requires the transfer to be completed.

question: List players who completed a transfer and then recorded match statistics in the same season.
selected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT "players"."player_id", "players"."first_name", "players"."last_name" FROM "players" INNER JOIN "player_match_stats" ON "players"."player_id" = "player_match_stats"."player_id" INNER JOIN "seasons" ON "player_match_stats"."season_id" = "seasons"."season_id" INNER JOIN "transfers" ON "players"."player_id" = "transfers"."player_id"
audit_reason: Never requires a completed transfer, same-season equality, or that the match appearance occurred after the transfer.

question: Show the top 10 teams by completed match wins.
selected_sql: SELECT t.team_id, t.team_name FROM teams t JOIN matches m ON t.team_id = m.winner_team_id GROUP BY t.team_id, t.team_name ORDER BY COUNT(m.match_id) DESC LIMIT 10
audit_reason: Ranks wins from all match statuses and never requires completed matches.

question: List teams that have both completed matches and active contracts.
selected_sql: SELECT t.team_id, t.team_name FROM teams t JOIN matches m ON t.team_id = m.home_team_id OR t.team_id = m.away_team_id JOIN contracts c ON t.team_id = c.team_id WHERE c.contract_status = 'active' GROUP BY t.team_id, t.team_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT m.match_id) > 0 AND COUNT(DISTINCT c.contract_id) > 0
audit_reason: Never filters matches to completed status.

question: List coaches who led training sessions but have no active team.
selected_sql: SELECT c.coach_id, c.first_name, c.last_name FROM coaches c JOIN training_sessions ts ON c.coach_id = ts.coach_id JOIN teams t ON c.team_id = t.team_id WHERE c.active_flag = '1' AND NOT EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM teams t2     WHERE t2.team_id = c.team_id     AND t2.active_flag = '1' )
audit_reason: Adds an unrequested active-coach restriction while the question only requires that the coach's team not be active.

question: List players who completed transfers but have no current active contract.
selected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT p.player_id, p.first_name, p.last_name FROM players p JOIN transfers t ON p.player_id = t.player_id JOIN contracts c ON p.player_id = c.player_id WHERE NOT EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM contracts c2     WHERE c2.player_id = p.player_id     AND c2.contract_status = 'active' )
audit_reason: Never filters completed transfers and the contract inner join excludes transferred players with no contracts.

question: List seasons with completed matches but no completed transfers.
selected_sql: SELECT s.season_id, s.season_name FROM seasons s JOIN matches m ON s.season_id = m.season_id WHERE NOT EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM transfers t     WHERE t.season_id = s.season_id ) GROUP BY s.season_id, s.season_name
audit_reason: Does not require completed matches and excludes seasons having any transfer rather than only completed transfers.

question: List players who trained for one team but played matches for another.
selected_sql: SELECT DISTINCT "players"."player_id", "players"."first_name", "players"."last_name" FROM "players" INNER JOIN "player_match_stats" ON "players"."player_id" = "player_match_stats"."player_id" INNER JOIN "player_training_stats" ON "players"."player_id" = "player_training_stats"."player_id"
audit_reason: Never compares the training team with the match team, so any player having both record types qualifies.

question: List leagues whose teams had both high-value and low-value players but no retired players.
selected_sql: SELECT l.league_id, l.league_name FROM leagues l JOIN teams t ON l.league_id = t.league_id JOIN players p ON t.team_id = p.team_id WHERE p.current_status != 'retired' GROUP BY l.league_id, l.league_name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN p.market_value >= (SELECT AVG(market_value) FROM players) THEN p.player_id END) > 0    AND COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN p.market_value < (SELECT AVG(market_value) FROM p
audit_reason: Filters retired players out before grouping, so it never proves that the league has no retired players.

question: List teams with completed matches, training sessions, and transfers but no injury records.
selected_sql: SELECT t.team_id, t.team_name FROM teams t JOIN matches m ON t.team_id = m.home_team_id JOIN training_sessions ts ON t.team_id = ts.team_id JOIN transfers tr ON t.team_id = tr.to_team_id WHERE NOT EXISTS (     SELECT 1     FROM injuries i     WHERE i.team_id = t.team_id ) GROUP BY t.team_id, t.team_name
audit_reason: Checks only home matches and incoming transfers and never requires the matches to be completed.

question: List players whose completed transfer fees exceed the average transferred player.
selected_sql: SELECT p.player_id, p.first_name, p.last_name FROM players p JOIN transfers t ON p.player_id = t.player_id GROUP BY p.player_id, p.first_name, p.last_name HAVING SUM(t.transfer_fee) > (SELECT AVG(total_fee) FROM (SELECT SUM(transfer_fee) AS total_fee FROM transfers GROUP BY player_id))
audit_reason: Never filters transfer_status = 'completed'.
